platform.name: PulseOps V2
platform.welcomeSubtitle: Modular operations platform with hot-drop micro-frontends
platform.noModuleSelected: Select a module from the navigation to get started.

auth.loginSubtitle: Enter your credentials to access the platform
auth.sessionExpired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.

login.failed: Login failed. Please check your credentials.
login.networkError: Unable to connect to the server. Please check if the API is running.

login.title: Welcome Back
login.subtitle: Sign in to your account
login.validationError: Both email and password are required

dashboard.title: Platform Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: System health, module status, and quick actions

moduleManager.title: Module Manager
moduleManager.subtitle: Install, enable, disable, and manage platform modules

logs.title: System Logs
logs.subtitle: Real-time platform logs, API calls, and diagnostics

logSettings.title: Log Storage Settings
logSettings.subtitle: Configure where platform logs are stored
logSettings.fileModeDesc: Logs are written to JSON files in the API project's Logs folder. Default mode.
logSettings.dbModeDesc: Logs are persisted to the PostgreSQL database for centralized querying.
logSettings.dbNotReadyDesc: Database must be configured and initialized before enabling database logging.

authSettings.title: Authentication Settings
authSettings.subtitle: Configure authentication provider for the platform

json_file.label: JSON File
json_file.description: Authenticate against a local JSON file. Default mode — works without a database.
json_file.detail: Ideal for initial setup and development before database is configured.

database.label: Database
database.description: Authenticate against the PostgreSQL system_users table with bcrypt hashing.
database.detail: Requires the database to be initialized with default data. Switch here after setup.

social.label: Social / OAuth2 / SAML
social.description: Authenticate via Google, Microsoft, or other OAuth2/SAML providers.
social.detail: Requires OAuth2/SAML provider configuration. Available in a future release.

authSettings.dbNotReadyWarning: Database must be initialized before switching to Database authentication.
authSettings.dbRequiredNote: Set up the database in Settings → Database Objects first.

switchProvider.confirmMessage: This will change the authentication provider for the entire platform. All users will authenticate using the new provider.

logs.emptyHint: Logs will appear here as you interact with the platform.

apiCalls.emptyHint: API requests and responses will appear here.

errors.networkError: Unable to connect to the server. Please check your connection.
errors.unauthorized: You are not authorized to perform this action.
errors.notFound: The requested resource was not found.
errors.serverError: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
errors.moduleLoadFailed: Failed to load module. Please try again or contact support.

databaseObjects.title: Database Objects
databaseObjects.subtitle: Manage database, schema, and default data

database.title: Database
database.createDesc: Create the PostgreSQL database if it does not exist.
database.deleteDesc: Drop the entire database. This is destructive and cannot be undone.

schema.title: Schema & Tables
schema.createDesc: Create the core schema and tables (system_users, system_config, system_modules, system_logs).
schema.wipeDesc: Drop all tables in the schema. Data will be permanently lost.
